instructions.instruction: Validate a Makefile using `bash scripts/validate_makefile.sh Makefile`
Use GNU make `-n --dry-run` for syntax validation; catches errors with line numbers
instructions.original_snippets: - **Syntax**: GNU make `-n --dry-run` validation; catches errors with line numbers
instructions.relevant_when: Checking a Makefile for syntax errors

instructions.instruction: Use mbake for tab indentation validation and formatting
instructions.original_snippets:
- **Formatting**: mbake tab indentation, variable assignment consistency, trailing whitespace, line continuations
...
[MBAKE VALIDATION]
âś“ mbake validation passed

instructions.instruction: Never use spaces instead of tabs for recipe indentation â€” GNU Make requires hard tab characters
instructions.original_snippets:
### NEVER use spaces instead of tabs for recipe indentation
- **WHY**: GNU Make requires a hard tab character to start recipe lines. Spaces silently produce `missing separator` errors.
- **DETECTION**: `scripts/validate_makefile.sh` catches this; run `make -n` to surface the error.

instructions.instruction: Never omit `.PHONY` for non-file targets (clean, test, build, all, etc.)
instructions.original_snippets:
### NEVER omit `.PHONY` for non-file targets
- **WHY**: Without `.PHONY`, if a file named `clean` or `test` exists, Make will silently skip the target.
- **GOOD**: `.PHONY: clean test build all` declared at the top
instructions.relevant_when: Any Makefile with targets that don't produce output files

instructions.instruction: Never use bare `$(shell ...)` calls or unquoted variable expansion in `rm`/`sudo`/`curl`/`wget` â€” unsafe variable expansion enables command injection
instructions.original_snippets:
### NEVER use bare `$(shell ...)` calls in recipe variables without quoting
- **WHY**: Unquoted shell variable expansion in `rm`, `sudo`, or `curl` commands enables command injection when variable values contain spaces or special characters.
- **BAD**: `rm -rf $(DIR)` when `DIR` can be user-controlled
- **GOOD**: `rm -rf "$(DIR)"` or validate that `DIR` does not contain path separators
instructions.relevant_when: Any Makefile recipe that uses rm, sudo, curl, wget with variable expansion

instructions.instruction: Never use bare `make` for recursive calls â€” always use `$(MAKE)`
instructions.original_snippets:
### NEVER use recursive `make` with a bare `make` command
- **WHY**: Bare `make` in a recipe does not inherit the jobserver flags passed by the parent make, breaking parallel builds.
- **BAD**: `make -C subdir`
- **GOOD**: `$(MAKE) -C subdir`
instructions.relevant_when: Any Makefile with recursive sub-directory builds

instructions.instruction: Never use `.EXPORT_ALL_VARIABLES` globally â€” use explicit `export VAR` only for variables that need sub-process visibility
instructions.original_snippets:
### NEVER export all variables globally with `.EXPORT_ALL_VARIABLES`
- **WHY**: Every variable in scope (including secrets loaded from `.env` files) gets exported to every sub-process, creating credential leakage risk.
- **BAD**: `.EXPORT_ALL_VARIABLES:` at top of Makefile
- **GOOD**: Use explicit `export VAR` only for variables that need sub-process visibility
instructions.relevant_when: Any Makefile that exports variables to sub-processes

instructions.instruction: Check for hardcoded credentials in Makefile (the validator automatically checks for these)
instructions.original_snippets:
- **Security**: hardcoded credentials, unsafe variable expansion in `rm`/`sudo`/`curl`/`wget`, command injection, `.EXPORT_ALL_VARIABLES` leakage
...
The validator automatically checks for hardcoded credentials

instructions.instruction: Use `mbake format --diff Makefile` to preview formatting changes before applying
instructions.original_snippets:
# Preview changes
mbake format --diff Makefile

# Apply formatting
mbake format Makefile

instructions.instruction: For legacy Makefile conversion: validate, fix critical errors, apply mbake format, add .PHONY with --auto-insert-phony-declarations, re-validate
instructions.original_snippets:
### Scenario 3: Converting Legacy Makefiles
```bash
# 1. Validate current state
bash scripts/validate_makefile.sh legacy.mk

# 2. Fix critical errors (tabs, syntax), then apply formatting
mbake format legacy.mk

# 3. Add .PHONY declarations
mbake format --auto-insert-phony-declarations legacy.mk

# 4. Re-validate
bash scripts/validate_makefile.sh legacy.mk
```
instructions.relevant_when: Modernizing or converting a legacy Makefile

instructions.instruction: mbake is automatically installed in an isolated venv per invocation â€” no manual installation required
instructions.original_snippets: **mbake** is automatically installed in an isolated venv per invocation and cleaned up on exit â€” no manual installation required.
instructions.relevant_when: Setting up to run mbake for the first time

instructions.instruction: mbake may false-positive on valid GNU Make special targets (.DELETE_ON_ERROR, .SUFFIXES, .ONESHELL, .POSIX) â€” the validator filters these
instructions.original_snippets: mbake doesn't recognize some valid GNU Make special targets (`.DELETE_ON_ERROR`, `.SUFFIXES`, `.ONESHELL`, `.POSIX`) â€” the validator filters these false positives and surfaces them as informational messages.
instructions.relevant_when: When validating Makefiles that use GNU Make special targets

instructions.instruction: Use `.DELETE_ON_ERROR` as a best practice to clean up partial output files
instructions.original_snippets: - **Best practices**: `.DELETE_ON_ERROR`, `.PHONY` declarations, `$(MAKE)` for recursive calls, `.ONESHELL` error handling
instructions.relevant_when: Makefiles where targets generate output files
